query: Sure, my phone number is 555-123-2002 and my name is John Smith. Let me know if you need anything else to get started.

state.history_summary:
assistant: Hi! How can I help you today?
user: Reports that the phone has shown “No Service” for hours and wants help restoring calls and mobile data.
assistant: Asked for account-identifying information such as the phone number.
user: Provided phone number 555-123-2002 and name John Smith.

candidate_answers.text: Thanks — I’ll look up the account tied to 555-123-2002 so I can identify the affected line and start troubleshooting the service issue.
The user has now supplied the phone number needed for an account lookup, so the grounded next step is to retrieve the customer record before checking line-level details or suggesting device actions.

provenance.source_type: manual annotation from real tau2 episode
provenance.decision_rule: Choose an early user turn where the caller has just provided the phone number requested for identity and line lookup, making customer retrieval the most grounded next action.
provenance.scoring_method: Manual one-step proxy scoring from the visible state. Scores favor account identification first and penalize line-specific or remediation actions that would require IDs or diagnostics not yet retrieved.
notes: Only the visible user query is used; no hidden account fields are imported into the state.
This sample anchors a telecom opening lookup step for domain balance and contrasts with later troubleshooting samples.
